title: Ayenia filipes
intro: Ayenia filipes, also known as the "Wild Sensitive Plant," is a plant species belonging to the Malpighiaceae family, characterized by its unique, sensitive leaves that fold up when touched. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ayenia filipes include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ayenia filipes is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
